reasoning: Historically, Zimbabwe has struggled heavily with implementing its compensation to white farmers, culminating in a drastically reduced initial cash payout in April 2025 (only 1% of the total amount for the first batch, equal to $3.1M). The remaining 99% was finalized as 10-year Treasury bonds that pay semi-annual interest. Given that the semi-annual interest wouldn't be due until October 2025—and given the lack of any news reports pointing to a sequential or 'second batch' cash payout between April 10 and June 1, 2025—an additional payment within that tight 50-day window is highly unlikely. The government's fiscal limitations and the fact that it took years to issue the first payment heavily suppress the probability of a rapid follow-up.
